primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup monetary items (dividend receivables) for designation as hedged items in a foreign currency hedge under IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs which items qualify as eligible hedged items for hedge accounting, including restrictions on intragroup items and requirements for forecast transactions
Specifically addresses foreign exchange treatment of intragroup monetary items and their exposure to currency fluctuations in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies scope of foreign currency risks that can qualify for hedge accounting in net investment hedges and the distinction between different parent-functional currency relationships

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosure requirements for designated hedging relationships, not relevant to eligibility determination
Cash flow classification and disclosure requirements, not relevant to hedge accounting eligibility
Addresses measurement of dividend payables, not hedging eligibility for foreign exchange risk

treatment_families.family: Hedging foreign exchange risk on recognized intragroup monetary items
Hedging foreign exchange risk on forecast intragroup transactions
Hedging net investment in foreign operation

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for recognized intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.1 permits recognized assets and liabilities as hedged items; IAS 21 para 45 explicitly recognizes that intragroup monetary items create foreign exchange exposure that must be reflected in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to transactions with parties external to reporting entity, which may preclude hedge designation for intragroup items despite IAS 21 recognizing their FX exposure

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.3 and 6.3.5 permit forecast intragroup transactions as hedged items when highly probable and denominated in currency other than entity's functional currency affecting consolidated P&L

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 clarifies that foreign currency risk on net assets of foreign operation can be hedged, which may apply when intragroup dividends relate to net investment disposal timing
